user_symptom: Customers will experience increased latency. Query (and other) jobs will timeout, or fail.

Affected customers experienced elevated error rates and latency when connecting to Google APIs. Existing workloads (i.e. running instances on GCE, or containers on GKE) were not impacted unless they needed to invoke impacted APIs.

Affected customers in Europe were unable to load the console, or experienced timeouts. Affected customers were unable to connect to the service using the API in europe-west1. Affected customers in Europe experienced unavailable errors and latency for both data and admin operations. Service was unavailable in europe-west1 and europe-west4. Affected customers experienced issues receiving notifications in asia-southeast1. Affected customers experienced probe creation request failures in europe-west1, europe-west4, and europe-west5. Affected customers were unable to create triggers in europe-west1. Affected customers experienced elevated errors in europe-west1.

Increased error rate. Google Front End (GFE) prevented API requests from reaching the service. Cloud Firestore saw 80% of listen streams become disconnected and a 50% drop in query/get requests. Cloud Logging was unavailable for viewing in the Cloud Console. Cloud Monitoring experienced elevated error rates. Cloud Compute Engine requests saw an increase in 502 errors. Cloud Spanner experienced elevated latency spikes and connection timeouts. Google Cloud SQL had minor connectivity problems and delayed instance log reporting. Firebase Crashlytics imports from AWS were impacted.

Google Cloud Monitoring experienced errors querying monitoring data for approximately 1 hour and 45 minutes. Customers may have experienced errors or incomplete monitoring data. Affected customers may see errors when trying to query their monitoring data.
Cloud Networking traffic going through us-central1 and us-east1 shows increased latency.
APIs, Credentials, Monitoring, IAM and several other pages on the Cloud console are loading slowly and may end up as a loading failure.
Cloud SQL has been affected by the Google incident. We are experiencing an issue with Cloud SQL.
Google Compute Engine customers experiencing elevated rates of packet loss in southamerica-east1-[a, b, c] due to the ongoing Cloud Networking issue.
Slow or stuck operations for zonal PD-Standard devices in us-west1-b.

Google Cloud Console - Affected customers in Europe were unable to load the console, or experienced timeouts. Cloud Bigtable - Affected customers in Europe experienced unavailable errors and latency for both data and admin operations. Cloud Spanner - Affected customers in Europe experienced unavailable errors and latency for both data and admin operations. Cloud Endpoints - Service was unavailable in europe-west1 and europe-west4. Firebase Messaging - Affected customers experienced issues receiving notifications in asia-southeast1. Cloud SQL - Affected customers experienced probe creation request failures in europe-west1, europe-west4, and europe-west5. Cloud Eventarc - Affected customers were unable to create triggers in europe-west1. App Engine - Affected customers experienced elevated errors in europe-west1.

service_name: Google Workspace and Google Cloud Platform
user_symptom: Multiple Google Workspace and Google Cloud Platform services experienced elevated error rates. Affected users experienced HTTP 500 Internal Server Errors. Customers experienced elevated API traffic error rates, generic unavailable errors, and elevated latency.
Significant packet loss on Cloud Interconnect in europe-west2 for some customers.

Google BigQuery service performance experienced an increase in latency for queries in multiple regions in the US for 82 hours. Queries exhibited higher than normal latency. Customers were unable to fully utilize their reserved slots.

Affected customers may have experienced delayed log entries in Log Explorer or other export destinations for the duration of the incident. Log entries written in europe-west3 are delayed by 1+ hours. They may not show up in Log Explorer or other export destinations. Customers would experience a delay in logs visibility.

Google Cloud customers using VMWare Engine experienced connectivity issues while using vSphere, vCenter and NSX in us-central1a zone for a duration of 6 hours, 23 minutes. Affected Customers may have observed issues with network partitions and accessing workload VMs. Affected Customers may have experienced connectivity issues using vCenter, HCX, NSX Manager, and NSX-T Edge virtual machines.
Cloud Logging logs-based metrics data points may be missing or delayed
Cloud logs might be unavailable for 25% of customers in global location. Logs won't be available, high latency when reading data.
Cloud Pub/Sub experiencing issues with seeing monitoring metric data, especially delivery_latency_health_score. Other metrics data may also be affected.

Customers using CNAME chasing may experience an issue with Cloud DNS. Querying for a record defined in one private zone that points to a record in another private zone does not return the final answer.

On Tuesday 21 March 2017, new connections to Cloud Pub/Sub experienced high latency leading to timeouts and elevated error rates for a duration of 95 minutes. Connections established before the start of this issue were not affected.

Google Cloud Load Balancing (GCLB) experienced increased TLS handshake errors or connection terminations for HTTPS traffic served from load balancers in Western Europe. HTTP load balancing returning error for some user in Europe.
Users experiencing failure with file uploads. The case attachment feature has been disabled until the issue is resolved.
Customers may not be able to look at live logs by running "tail" commands

Customers using Google Cloud Networking experienced a network traffic disruption in us-central1, us-east1, us-west1 regions on 22 September 2022 for 8 minutes starting 04:30 US/Pacific and for 10 minutes starting 04:48 US/Pacific (total duration of 18 minutes). Some customers using Cloud Networking experienced severe traffic disruption for the two occurrences of the incident. Some cloud customers communicating outside the affected regions (including to the Internet) would have seen two periods of disruption, ~8 minutes at 04:30 AM, ~10 minutes at 04:48 AM US/Pacific.

Containers within GKE cluster node pools using docker are getting restarted in the event of docker restarts. GKE cluster pods restart when docker restarts. Customers may see containers restarting when docker daemon is restarted.

Some services in us-east1-c and us-east1-d are failing, customers impacted by this incident would likely experience a total unavailability of zonal services hosted in us-east1-c or us-east1-d. Connections to and from VMs in us-east1-c and us-east1-d may fail.
preventing customers from creating or updating Support cases. Cloud Console pages that are nested under https://console.cloud.google.com/support may have also failed to load or update.
Attempts to create a new instance or to delete an instance fail with an error. Instances created may experience DNS propagation delays.
This may appear as timeouts, 5XX errors and potential authorization errors being returned from the service.
Customers may see containers restarting when docker daemon is restarted. Customers may see GKE Cluster pods restarting when Docker restarts.

Customers impacted by this issue would have experienced abnormal instance terminations for instances which were running in the impacted building. VPCs in zone europe-west2-a were inaccessible. Customers also experienced 100% packet loss to zone europe-west2-a. Customers were unable to create PD devices in the impacted zone. Instances in the impacted zone were terminated, and therefore weren’t able to access their PD devices.

Cloud SQL External Server or Database Migration Service (DMS) users who migrate to Cloud SQL MySQL 5.7 with GTID replication may experience errors if they have insert/update/delete transactions during the dump phase. Furthermore, if updates were made to those jobs during the replication phase, the migration job may fail.

Google Cloud Networking experienced increased latency, packet loss, and service unavailable errors for traffic between regions and from Google to external endpoints including on-premises environments and the internet.
Connectivity issues in us-central1 resulting in failures and elevated latency.
Starting from 16:40 US/Pacific time, users attempting to update or deploy Cloud Load Balancers may experience delays or failures.

Customers using CNAME chasing may experience an issue with Cloud DNS. Querying for a record defined in one private zone that points to a record in another private zone does not return the final answer.

Some services in us-east1-c and us-east1-d are failing, customers impacted by this incident would likely experience a total unavailability of zonal services hosted in us-east1-c or us-east1-d. Connections to and from VMs in us-east1-c and us-east1-d may fail.

Anthos On-Prem customers unable to login to admin workstation (starting 2021-08-25 for customers using Anthos OP v1.7.2). Diagnosis: Customers are unable to login to their admin workstation in both their prod and non-prod environments.

Customers attempting to create github triggers using a Cloud Console URL that ends in their project id will see a "Failed to load" error with the following message: "There was an error while loading /cloud-build/triggers?project= Please try again"

Customers may see containers restarting when docker daemon is restarted. Clusters may see pods restarting when Dockers are restarted. Customers may see GKE Cluster pods restarting when Docker restarts.
Some customers may be experiencing connection failures in Docker workflow to GCLB, GCB, or Fastly destinations such as debian.org, github.com with a timeout error.
We are experiencing increased tail latency for predictions on Vertex AI Online Prediction. Users might experience higher latency.

The issue with Cloud Composer environment creation/deletion errors has been resolved for all affected projects as of Thursday, 2019-07-25 18:31 US/Pacific. The issue with Cloud Composer environment creation/deletion errors should be resolved for the majority of projects. Currently, approximately 5% of the requests are still failing. We are investigating errors with creating and deleting Cloud Composer environments.
We are investigating an issue with GCE Networking in us-east4 region affecting GCE VMs, GKE, Cloud VPN and Cloud Private Interconnect resulting in network packet loss.
The issue with Google BigQuery API returning 503 errors has been resolved for all affected projects as of 1:30 US/Pacific.
Node pool creation failing in multiple zones
Networking issues in us-central1-c impacting multiple GCP products (Bigtable, Cloud SQL, Datastore, VMs)
We experiencing delays in showing logging.
Affected customers are unable to access all Stackdriver services via stackdriver.com, the Cloud Console, and the API.
StackDriver Trace API is experiencing an elevated rate of request failures.
The issue with Google Stackdriver Logging has been resolved for all affected projects.
The issue with Google Stackdriver Logging has been resolved for all affected projects.
Cloud SQL First Generation instances unavailable in us-central1-a
The issue with Cloud Networking increased packet loss into and out of southamerica-east1.
Google Kubernetes Engine Node Pool creation through Cloud Console UI.
Google Stackdriver Alerts not firing reliably
Current data indicate that all GKE API requests in region europe-west4 are failing.
Creation of new google cloud functions and update of current one are failing
The issue with Google Cloud Networking has been resolved for all affected users.

Allocation of new external IP addresses for GCE instance creation continues to be unavailable in us-east4. We are experiencing an issue with Google Cloud Networking and Google Compute Engine in us-east4 that prevents the creation of GCE instances that require allocation of new external IP addresses. We are experiencing an issue with Google Cloud Networking and Google Compute Engine in us-east4 that prevents the creation of GCE instances with external IP addresses attached.
Affected customers are unable to access all Stackdriver services via stackdriver.com, the Cloud Console, and the API.
The issue with Google Cloud Networking intermittent traffic disruption to and from us-central has been resolved for all affected users as of 2018-02-23 22:35 US/Pacific.
The Google Compute Engine service is experiencing delays in network programming in us-east1.
We are investigating timeouts with Google Cloud Functions deployments.
Users may see slow performance or intermittent errors.

Starting at Tuesday 27 June 2017 07:30 PST, Google Cloud Storage started experiencing degraded availability for some objects based in us-central1 buckets (Regional, Nearline, Coldline, Durable Reduced Availability) and US multi-region buckets. Between 08:00 and 18:00 PST error rate was ~3.5%, error rates have since decreased to 0.5%. Errors are expected to be consistent for objects.

During the period 12:05 - 13:57 PDT, GCS requests originating in Europe experienced a 17% error rate. The issue with elevated latency and error rates for GCS in Europe should be resolved as of 13:56 PDT. We are continuing to investigate elevated errors and latency for GCS in Europe. We are currently investigating elevated latency and error rates for Google Cloud Storage traffic transiting through Europe.
Stackdriver Uptime Monitoring experienced a period of unavailability on 2017-05-16 between 10:20 and 10:58 AM Pacific time, which has been resolved for all affected projects.
We are experiencing an issue with packet loss for Google Cloud VPN, Cloud Interconnect, and Cloud Router beginning at Monday, 2018-10-22 13:42 US/Pacific.
We've received a report of an issue with Google App Engine.
The issue with Google Kubernetes Engine errors when viewing clusters on the dashboard has been resolved for all affected projects.
Stackdriver Logging Service Degraded
Support Center inaccessible
The issue with accessing Support Center has been resolved for all affected users.
Current data indicates traffic to other regions may see approximately 12% packet loss.
The issue with Google Kubernetes Engine has been resolved for all affected projects.
Cloud Storage showing inconsistent results for object listing for multi-regional buckets in US.
OAuth login issues
Google Cloud Engine VM instances losing connectivity
users are unable to create new support cases
We are investigating loss for traffic to and from South America.
App Engine Admin-API experiencing high error rates

The issue with Cloud SQL V2 incorrect reports of 'Unable to Failover' state has been resolved for all affected instances as of 12:00 US/Pacific. The issue with Cloud SQL V2 some instance maybe failing to create should be resolved for some of projects and we expect a full resolution in the near future. We are experiencing an issue with Cloud SQL V2, some instance maybe failing to create or HA instances may reports incorrect 'Unable to Failover' state beginning at Thursday, 2016-06-29 08:45 US/Pacific.
Affected customers are unable to access all Stackdriver services via stackdriver.com, the Cloud Console, and the API.
The issue with Google Compute Engine has been resolved for all affected projects as of 20:30 US/Pacific.
Google BigQuery jobs failing with an "Internal Error" message.
We are investigating errors with Firebase Test Lab. We will be pausing all test execution until root cause is found and mitigated.
Unable to browse GCS storage buckets in Google Console
High latency and failures on queries and deployments.

We are experiencing an issue with Google Cloud Storage service returning elevated error rates for requests in the US multi-region, starting at Friday, 2018-12-21 08:06 US/Pacific. Google Cloud Storage service is reporting a 1% error rate for all requests. Affected Google Cloud Functions customers may seen their deployments time out. Affected customers of Google Cloud Build were observable as "Build failed (internal error)".
Current data indicates that approximately 60% of the Cloud SQL admin operations globally, including instance creation, are affected by the recent Cloud PubSub outage.
We are investigating a potential issue with Stackdriver Monitoring affecting the us-west2 region.
We are investigating an issue with Cloud PubSub.
We've received a report of an issue with Cloud Dataflow.

We are experiencing an issue with Google Cloud Storage service returning elevated error rates for requests in the US multi-region. Google Cloud Storage service is reporting a 1% error rate for all requests. Affected Google Cloud Functions customers may see their deployments time out. Affected customers of Google Cloud Build were observable as 'Build failed (internal error)'.
Datastore experiencing elevated error rates
Users are unable to create new support cases.
We are experiencing an issue with Memcache availability beginning at November 6, 2017 at 12:30 pm US/Pacific. Current data indicate(s) that all projects using Memcache are affected by this issue.
Elevated errors are occurring intermittently across many BQ APIs, but most common in TableService, DatasetService, and JobService operations.
We've received a report of an issue with Cloud Dataproc in us-central1.
We've received a report of an issue with Google Cloud Function deployments seeing increased errors.
Intermittent errors for requests to the BigQuery API in europe-west2.

Customers without an organization are unable to create projects through the Enablement API. Customers without an organization may receive an error message: "You may not have permissions to create projects in this organization".
We are experiencing an issue with packet loss for Google Cloud VPN, Cloud Interconnect, and Cloud Router beginning at Monday, 2018-10-22 13:42 US/Pacific.

Google Compute Engine, Google Cloud VPN, and Google Cloud Network Load Balancers hosted in the us-east4 region experienced 100% packet loss from the internet and other GCP regions. Google Dedicated Interconnect Attachments located in us-east4 also experienced loss of connectivity.
Google Kubernetes Engine may clear certain add-ons from the configuration UI after a cluster upgrade.
Jobs using Dataflow Shuffle may fail or (when using Flexible Resource Scheduling) be queued indefinitely.
Issue affecting the ability to list projects and organizations
preventing the creation of new GCLB load balancers and updating of existing ones
Customers affected by this issue may see an error similar to the following: ERROR: (gcloud.functions.deploy) OperationError: code=3, message=Build failed.

We are experiencing an issue with Cloud Router monitoring data (metrics) missing beginning at Monday, 2020-12-14 12:00 US/Pacific. Diagnosis: Visible decrease in Cloud Router data in Metrics Explorer.
All the components that require access to storage infrastructure may be affected by this issue in multiple regions.
Autoscaling of instances in Managed Instance Groups may not occur due to instances being unreachable.

Jobs using Dataflow Shuffle may fail or (when using Flexible Resource Scheduling) be queued indefinitely. If you have a job in an unhealthy state, either failed or queued for more than 6 hours, please restart it so that it is executed correctly.
Customers are not able to create new databases in Cloud Firestore.
Customers peering with us-west2 may experience packet loss.
Google BigQuery jobs failing with an "Internal Error" message.
We experiencing delays in showing logging.
We are experiencing an issue with increased system lag in some Google Cloud Dataflow jobs.
Affected customers may experience an elevated error rate (up to 100%) when submitting dataset insertion operations.
Google BigQuery users experiencing latency and high error rates in US multi region.
Customers may experience increased latency or connection timeouts between GCP and other Cloud Providers.

We are experiencing an intermittent issue with Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) cluster creation & deletion operations timing out in all regions. Affected customers may experience timeout errors upon cluster creation and deletion.
We are experiencing an intermittent issue with Cloud Firestore beginning at Friday, 2020-12-04 12:09:08 US/Pacific.
We are investigating errors with Firebase Test Lab. We will be pausing all test execution until root cause is found and mitigated.
Cloud SQL instances in europe-west2-a may be unreachable
We are investigating an issue with Google Kubernetes Engine node pool creation through Cloud Console UI.
reduced URLFetch availability
Customers may observe their calico node pods failing to start and crash-looping indefinitely on GKE 1.24.7+ and 1.25.3+.

Cloud Memory Store users experienced sporadic levels of inability to create new instances from 15 Dec 2022 at 18:30 PT until 19 Dec 2022 16:00, when the failure rate reached 100%. Affected customers were unable to create new instances, as the region field in the user interface (UI) could not be populated due to a metadata issue.
Cloud Functions Gen 2 customers are missing metrics and experiencing incorrect billing for their SKUs.

Google Compute Engine customers running “yum” or “dnf” commands in Red Hat instances experienced 403 forbidden responses when attempting to install packages. 100 % of customers running “yum” in RHEL instances received a 403 - Forbidden response. All Red Hat instances attempting to get updates encountered failures for the duration of the outage.
MemoryStore for Redis - instances update/export failures

Google Cloud Networking experienced intermittent packet loss for traffic between multiple cloud regions for a duration of 8 minutes. Customers may have observed packet loss for traffic routed via transpacific links on Google's backbone. Customers may have experienced up to 40% packet loss between VMs in the following affected regions.
ssh fails with ERROR: (gcloud.compute.start-iap-tunnel) Error while connecting [4047: 'Failed to lookup instance']
Global: Calico enabled GKE clusters’ pods may get stuck Terminating or Pending after upgrading to 1.22+
Intermittent failures (ERROR: PERMISSION_DENIED: The caller does not have permission) when trying to list/describe the OAuth client via gCloud or Terraform.
Customers may see failures while attempting to update/export their basic tier instances.

Affected customers may have experienced: - All pre-existing Vertex models undeployed on Vertex AI Endpoints - Empty responses when listing the deployed models - Runtime exceptions and general errors on Predict and Explain requests - Quota failure when trying to re-deploy models

Google BigQuery experienced elevated query latencies and job failures globally for a duration of 18 hours, 15 minutes. Affected customers would have experienced elevated query latencies or in some cases query failures.

Customers were unable to create or edit Google Compute Engine (GCE) instances via the Google Cloud Console. Affected customers experienced failures creating or editing GCE instances via the Google Cloud Console and may have received an error “Constraint constraints/compute.vmExternalIpAccess violated for project [project ID].“

Customers were unable to access their Cloud Volume Service (CVS) storage across GCP Regions. Customers may have faced issues with traffic routing in some cases where dynamic routes were not propagated appropriately on peered networks across regions.

Google Infrastructure Configuration Server operation requests failing; External HTTP/S Load Balancing, Cloud CDN - config changes will be accepted & fail to propagate; Cloud Armor rules may also be affected; Appengine flex customers may see apps fail to deploy; Traffic Director may see configs fail to propagate; VM instance group healthchecks & functionality like autoscaling/autohealing may be affected

Customer may experience replication failure on the new CSA rollout on replica, if the master instance is busy with INSERTs/UPDATEs on tables with timestamp column and session timezone is set to other than SYSTEM.
NaN
Customers may observe deployment failures and timeout errors.

Artifact Registry: Affected customers would not have been able to create new repositories in the Asia multi-region. Customers would have received a failure message of “Request contains an invalid argument.” Google Cloud Composer: Affected customers in the Asia multi-region were not able to create Composer environments, receiving errors indicating either an issue with AppEngine or Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) cluster creation. Google Cloud Storage: Affected customers would have experienced increased error rates when creating buckets in the Asia multi-region. Google Kubernetes Engine: Affected customers would have been unable to create new clusters in the affected regions.

Google Cloud App Engine customers might have experienced elevated errors while using the Secrets Manager for a duration of 6 days, 10 hours and 46 minutes. Customers might have experienced increased errors from apps or functions that use the Secrets Manager.
NaN

Google Cloud Networking experienced increased packet loss for egress traffic from Google to the Middle East and elevated latency between our Europe and Asia Regions for 3 hours and 12 minutes. Customers using Cloud Load Balancing would have experienced increased packet loss up to 60% for egress traffic from Google to the Middle East and increased network latency between our Europe and Asia regions. Cloud Interconnect customers peering in the Middle East region would have experienced packet loss up to 80% during the incident. Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) customers requiring cross-regional traffic would have experienced packet loss of up to 50% and elevated network latency.

Customer configs propagation depending on the dos_cloud configs got delayed. Some impacted customers would not be able to make Cloud Armor config changes. Customer are unable to make cloud armour config changes globally.

Cloud Function 2nd Generation deletes are erroring out in multiple regions. Delete function operations are erroring with following message although the corresponding resources are getting deleted and customers are able to reuse the function name. Operation Failed. Public Error Status: code: 5 message: Delete package operation for projects/[PROJECT_ID]/locations/[REGION]/repositories/gcf-artifacts/packages/[FUNCTION_ID]%2Fcustom-run has failed. Additional information: Requested entity was not found.

Global: Increase in failure rate for SQLServer Instance Creation. Cloud SQL for SQL Server is seeing a spike in instance creation failures globally. Diagnosis: Increased failure rate for SQLServer instance creation.

Customers experienced a cloud networking disruption from 04:30 AM - 04:58 AM US/Pacific. Customers might have experienced a cloud networking disruption from 04:30 AM - 04:58 AM US/Pacific as a result of an issue on physical network. All cloud customers communicating outside the region (including to the internet) would have seen two periods of disruption, ~8m at 04:30 AM, ~10m at 04:48 AM US/Pacific.

Artifact Registry: Affected customers would not have been able to create new repositories in the Asia multi-region. Customers would have received a failure message of “Request contains an invalid argument.” Google Cloud Composer: Affected customers in the Asia multi-region were not able to create Composer environments, receiving errors indicating either an issue with AppEngine or Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) cluster creation. Google Cloud Storage: Affected customers would have experienced increased error rates when creating buckets in the Asia multi-region. Google Kubernetes Engine: Affected customers would have been unable to create new clusters in the affected regions.

On Tuesday, 13 September 2022 07:30 US/Pacific, an issue with Google Cloud Filestore resulted in all read-only API requests failing for a period of 3 hours, 12 minutes. As a result of this throttling, read-only API access was unavailable for all customers. This affected customers in all locations, due to a global quota that applies to Filestore. Console, gcloud and API access (List, GetOperation, etc.) calls all failed for a duration of 3 hours, 12 minutes. Customers encountered error 429 when listing Cloud Filestore instances using API, gcloud CLI, and Google Cloud Console UI.
Customers may experience latency and errors with Composer creation.

We are experiencing an issue with Google Cloud Bigtable Metrics. CPU Load Metrics are impacted and could show the CPU Load as zero in the impacted locations. Customers who are periodically scaling their clusters based on the CPU Load Metrics will be operating on wrong data. Replication metrics are also affected.
Customers using the Secrets Manager are experiencing increased errors. Customers are experiencing increased errors.
Summary: Cloud Build Retry Build action degraded. Diagnosis: Retry Build operations may fail with an error and the build will not be retried.
Global: Increase in failure rate for SQLServer Instance Creation. Cloud SQL for SQL Server is seeing a spike in instance creation failures globally.
NaN
503 error response can be observed for Google Cloud Functions. Cloud Scheduler jobs are failing due to 503 error from Google Cloud Functions.

On Thursday, 16 June 2022, customers were unable to create or edit Google Compute Engine (GCE) instances via the Google Cloud Console for 2 days, 20 hours, 31 minutes. Affected customers experienced failures creating or editing GCE instances via the Google Cloud Console and may have received an error “Constraint constraints/compute.instanceExternalIpAccess violated for project [project ID].“
Customers who are deploying GCFv2 functions and who have never enabled Cloud Run API before will observe failures with deployments.

Customers may see older jobs (finished more than 180 days ago) and jobs which are currently running disappear from the Console and API output. Jobs which are currently running will see data plane errors if the control plane job resource is deleted.
Datastore customers will experience increased errors and latency.
Customers using Local SSD on VMs could see the VM terminated unexpectedly, which shows in the console as an Instance Termination by Compute Engine.

Google Cloud Networking control plane experienced elevated latency. All Google Cloud Load Balancer config to Google Front End stalled globally for a duration of 53 minutes. Customers might have experienced issues while creating new forwarding rules for Global Layer 7 External load balancer or edit or delete existing ones.

Google Cloud Filestore experienced an issue where ListInstances API requests failed with error code 429 for a duration of 3 hours, 04 minutes. Customers encountered error 429 when listing Cloud Filestore instances using API, gcloud CLI, and Google Cloud Console UI.
Customers may experience elevated latency for inter-region in multiple asia regions.
